name: nginx-proxy-manager
description: Reverse proxy manager with a web UI, SSL certificates, and proxy host management

fields.name: NPM_HTTP_PORT
fields.label: Public HTTP Port
fields.help:
Public HTTP port used by Nginx Proxy Manager for proxy traffic and Let's Encrypt HTTP challenges.
Usually leave this as 80.

fields.name: NPM_HTTPS_PORT
fields.label: Public HTTPS Port
fields.help:
Public HTTPS port used by Nginx Proxy Manager for TLS traffic.
Usually leave this as 443.

fields.name: NPM_ADMIN_PORT
fields.label: Admin UI Port
fields.help:
Port used to open the Nginx Proxy Manager admin interface in your browser.
After deployment, visit this port to complete the first login.

fields.name: NPM_DATA_LOCATION
fields.label: Data Folder
fields.help: Stores Nginx Proxy Manager data, configuration, proxy hosts, access lists, and the internal SQLite database.

fields.name: NPM_LETSENCRYPT_LOCATION
fields.label: Let's Encrypt Folder
fields.help: Stores issued Let's Encrypt certificates and related SSL material.

fields.name: RESTART_POLICY
fields.label: Restart Policy
fields.help: Controls whether the container restarts automatically.

fields.name: NPM_IMAGE
fields.label: Image
fields.help:
Docker image used for Nginx Proxy Manager.
Change this only if you want a different tag or image source.

fields.name: NPM_DISABLE_IPV6
fields.label: Disable IPv6
fields.help: Set this to Yes only if IPv6 is not enabled on your host and the upstream documentation recommends disabling it.

fields.name: NPM_CONTAINER_NAME
fields.label: Container Name
fields.help: EasyDocker sets the container name from the project name.

fields.name: NPM_DATA_TARGET
fields.label: Data Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal path where Nginx Proxy Manager stores app data.

fields.name: NPM_LETSENCRYPT_TARGET
fields.label: Let's Encrypt Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal path where certificates are stored.

ui.overview: Nginx Proxy Manager gives you a web interface to publish local services behind a reverse proxy, manage SSL certificates, and control access without hand-writing Nginx config.
what_happens: An Nginx Proxy Manager container will be created
Public HTTP, HTTPS, and admin UI ports will be published on the host
Application data and Let's Encrypt certificates will be stored in the folders you choose
After deployment, open the admin UI and complete the first login and password change
The default first login is admin@example.com with password changeme, and Nginx Proxy Manager will ask you to change both
ui.data_location: Relative paths are stored inside this app's project folder under the mounted EasyDocker base config path.
